name: Civica and Civica waterfall - circular trailrunning along the Tajuña river
name_original: Cívica y cascada de Cívica — circular trailrunning por el río Tajuña
region_intro: Jogging around Brihuega offers a chance to explore a landscape characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and agricultural fields. The region features varied terrain, from the open expanses of lavender fields to paths alongside the Tajuña river. Elevation changes are generally moderate, with some routes including more significant climbs. Best jogging routes around Brihuega The most popular jogging route is Brihuega Lavender Fields Trail, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 25 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views through the famous lavender fields. Another top favourite among local runners is Circular trailrunning through the Peñascos de los Frailes from Las Inviernas, a moderate 7.4 miles (11.8 km) path. This route navigates through rocky outcrops, providing a varied running experience. Local runners also love the Civica and Civica waterfall - circular trailrunning along the Tajuña river, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and past a waterfall, often completed in about 1 hour 17 minutes. Jogging around Brihuega is defined by lavender fields, river paths, and rocky hills, offering a mix of natural beauty and varied terrain. The network includes options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 runners have used komoot to explore Brihuega's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Brihuega?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;13 dedicated jogging routes&lt;/strong&gt; detailed in this guide for the Brihuega area, offering a variety of experiences through its unique landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Brihuega?
A: While this guide primarily features moderate and difficult running routes, many of the moderate trails can be enjoyed by beginners with a reasonable level of fitness. There are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in this selection, but routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142658" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Torija&lt;/a&gt; offer a good starting point with less elevation gain.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running trails in Brihuega?
A: The running trails around Brihuega offer diverse scenery, from the famous &lt;strong&gt;lavender fields&lt;/strong&gt; to paths along the Tajuña river. You'll also encounter rocky outcrops, agricultural fields, and charming riverine landscapes. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032318863/brihuega-lavender-fields-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brihuega Lavender Fields Trail&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032328125/civica-and-civica-waterfall-circular-trailrunning-along-the-tajuna-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Civica and Civica waterfall - circular trailrunning along the Tajuña river&lt;/a&gt; are particularly scenic.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Brihuega area?
A: Yes, several routes in the guide are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032331605/circular-trailrunning-through-the-penascos-de-los-frailes-from-las-inviernas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular trailrunning through the Peñascos de los Frailes from Las Inviernas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032328125/civica-and-civica-waterfall-circular-trailrunning-along-the-tajuna-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Civica and Civica waterfall - circular trailrunning along the Tajuña river&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural features to see along the running routes?
A: You can discover several unique sights. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032328125/civica-and-civica-waterfall-circular-trailrunning-along-the-tajuna-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Civica and Civica waterfall - circular trailrunning along the Tajuña river&lt;/a&gt; route passes by the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cívica Rock Dwellings and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Another route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032331605/circular-trailrunning-through-the-penascos-de-los-frailes-from-las-inviernas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular trailrunning through the Peñascos de los Frailes from Las Inviernas&lt;/a&gt;, takes you through the distinctive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rocks of the Friars&lt;/a&gt;. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4440570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brihuega&lt;/a&gt; itself is a charming starting point for many runs.

Q: Is Brihuega a good place for family-friendly running or jogging?
A: The routes around Brihuega are generally moderate to difficult, with varied terrain and some elevation changes. While there are no routes specifically designated as 'easy' or 'family-friendly' in this guide, families with older children who are accustomed to longer distances and varied surfaces may enjoy some of the moderate options, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032318863/brihuega-lavender-fields-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brihuega Lavender Fields Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Brihuega?
A: Many natural trails in the Brihuega region are suitable for running with dogs, provided they are well-behaved and kept under control, especially near livestock or in populated areas. Always ensure your dog is fit enough for the chosen route and carry enough water for both of you. It's advisable to check local regulations for specific areas or nature reserves.

Q: What is the best season for running in Brihuega?
A: The best season for running in Brihuega largely depends on your preference. Spring and early summer are particularly beautiful, especially when the lavender fields are in bloom (typically June-July), offering vibrant colors and pleasant temperatures. Autumn also provides cooler weather and stunning fall foliage. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended, while winters are generally mild but can be chilly.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by a waterfall?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032328125/civica-and-civica-waterfall-circular-trailrunning-along-the-tajuna-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Civica and Civica waterfall - circular trailrunning along the Tajuña river&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice that leads directly past the picturesque Cívica waterfall, offering a refreshing highlight during your run.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for jogging routes in Brihuega?
A: The jogging routes in this guide vary in length, generally ranging from approximately 7 km (4.3 miles) to over 15 km (9.3 miles). Durations can range from just under an hour to over two hours, depending on the route's distance, elevation, and your running pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032318863/brihuega-lavender-fields-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brihuega Lavender Fields Trail&lt;/a&gt; is about 11.9 km and takes around 1 hour 25 minutes.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails?
A: Many routes start from or near towns and villages such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4440570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brihuega&lt;/a&gt; or Las Inviernas, where public parking is typically available. It's advisable to check the specific starting point of your chosen route on komoot for more detailed parking information, as options can vary.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Brihuega?
A: Runners frequently praise the diverse and scenic landscapes, particularly the vibrant lavender fields and the tranquil paths along the Tajuña river. The varied terrain, from rolling hills to rocky outcrops, also offers an engaging challenge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032318863/brihuega-lavender-fields-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brihuega Lavender Fields Trail&lt;/a&gt; is often highlighted for its unique beauty.
tips: Cívica does not go unnoticed in the eyes of those who travel the road and soon they see that the rock in front of their eyes has a door, railings and other architectural elements that attract a lot of attention. It is an abandoned town that was carved out of the rock like a fantasy movie and according to studies, its galleries were used since the Middle Ages.

Cívica is a fascinating village in the province of Guadalajara, known for its rock-cut structures, which have earned it the nickname of the "Cappadocia of Alcarria".
Cívica is a perfect place for lovers of history and nature.
